Recognizer replaces an `optional orelse fallback` fallback expression with `unreachable` (span covers the fallback), skips an existing `orelse unreachable`, excludes test bodies, and carries may_fail compile expectation; the survivor fixture (test/fixtures/mutators/optional/survivor.zig) documents the missing-null-path survivor case.

entries.notes: Preview mutator only; stable optional task may add fixtures but must not implement or enable it unless a future task names this operator.

Recognizer swaps equality comparisons against the null literal (operand-order aware: x==null, x!=null, null==x, null!=x) with compiles compile expectation; the comparison mutator continues to leave null comparisons to this operator, and non-null equality is not recognized here.

Recognizer replaces a `catch` handler (the catch right-hand side) with `unreachable`, preserving the exact handler span (for `catch |err| handler` only the handler is replaced, not the |err| capture), skips an existing `catch unreachable`, excludes test bodies, and carries may_fail compile expectation; killed/survived fixtures (test/fixtures/mutators/error_path/{killed,survivor}.zig) document the error-path outcomes.

Recognizer replaces a whole `errdefer <body>` statement with `errdefer {}` (an empty no-op error cleanup), spanning the errdefer keyword through the cleanup body; skips an existing `errdefer {}`, excludes test bodies, leaves `defer` unchanged (distinct node tag), and carries may_fail compile expectation (removing the only use of a captured resource can make it unused). Added to src/mutators/error_path.zig alongside error_catch_unreachable; killed/survived fixtures under test/fixtures/mutators/errdefer/.

entries.notes: Requires safety-mode matrix; task 058 may add fixture coverage but does not implement preview mutators by default.

Recognizer emits paired +1/-1 boundary candidates for plain-decimal integer literals that are direct operands of a comparison (==, !=, <, <=, >, >=), covering branch and length checks; candidate order is deterministic via the canonical replacement sort. Protected literals are rejected by the context allowlist (declarations, enum tags, array lengths, and align(...) are not comparison operands, so they are never examined); non-decimal (hex/binary/octal/underscore) literals are skipped as syntax-local; test bodies are excluded; compile expectation is may_fail because +/-1 can overflow the operand type.
Needs loop and range fixture coverage.
